destinations.evidence: Settlement transaction construction obtains executor, commitment, inclusion, and total amounts from calculate_async_fees rather than separate local fee branches.
The shared entry point returns the complete fee breakdown consumed by the payload builder.

equivalence_argument: The payload builder delegates settlement fee construction to the same calculate_async_fees entry point used by verification, including error propagation.
notes: Mapped to the port payload-builder settlement path.
rationale: The payload builder removes its separate HTTP/LLM fee branches and calls `calculate_async_fees`, while the commit body explicitly says block building was unified with the verification entry point. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: Async settlement transaction construction must calculate executor, commitment, inclusion, and total fees through `calculate_async_fees`, so block building uses the same fee logic as verification.
suggested_falsification_tests: For representative HTTP and LLM async metadata, construct settlement fees in the payload builder and independently call `calculate_async_fees` with the same precompile address, input, and output; assert executor, commitment, inclusion, and total fees are identical, and assert a `calculate_async_fees` error prevents settlement construction.
summary: Settlement construction uses the shared async fee calculation entry point.
test_or_falsification_candidate: For HTTP and LLM settlement metadata, compare payload-builder fee fields with an independent calculate_async_fees call and assert errors prevent settlement construction.
title: Settlement fee calculation uses the shared async fee entry point
